betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Watz Operation & Waste§f

§aOperate your Watz reactor efficiently and handle the toxic byproducts safely!§f

§6GUI Layout§f:
§b• Fuel Pellet Assembly§f — holds pellets; not location-dependent
§b• Fluid Gauges§f — coolant, hot coolant, poisonous mud (§e64,000 mB§f each)
§b• Pellet Configuration Lock§f — maintains pellet layout when auto-replacing
§b• Heat Gauge§f — needle display; end of gauge ≠ max value or meltdown risk
§b• Flux Value§f — current neutron flux level

§6Operation§f:
§b• Redstone signal§f to pressure pump starts the reaction
§b• Cold coolant§f in through top ports
§b• Hot coolant + poisonous mud§f out through bottom ports
§b• No heat generation cap§f beyond the gauge display — purely visual

§6Fuel Management§f:
§b• Depletes §eextremely slowly§f — good for servers, impractical for breeding
§b• Absorber pellets increase poisonous mud production by consuming flux
§b• Fuel options: MEU, MEP, HEN, and schrabidium-based pellets

§6Fuel Reprocessing§f:
§b• Spent pellets are cold and non-radioactive — safe to handle!
§b• Centrifuge directly for §btechnetium§f, §bbismuth§f, and §beuphemium§f
§b• Slow depletion makes this impractical as a primary breeding strategy

§6Meltdown§f:
§b• Triggered when §bpoisonous mud buffer§f fills to maximum
§b• Violent explosion irradiating a wide area + falling fallout
§b• Spews corrosive poisonous mud that damages blocks like corium
§b• Among the §eworst meltdowns§f if not properly contained

§6Pros§f: Stackable (single signal), simple operation, cool model, safe spent fuel.
§6Cons§f: Least efficient late-game fission reactor (≈ZIRNOX level), incredibly expensive, low power (~5 MHE/s), aggressive meltdown, waste liquid builds up at §e3,000 mB/s§f on high-power fuels.

§eTip§f: Solidify poisonous mud immediately! Automate the output with fluiducts and a solidifier to prevent catastrophic buffer overflow.
